Farn Polrrrcal PnacrrcEs CouurssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 r Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
December 30,2003
Linda M. Balok
Steefel, Levitt & Weiss
One Embarcadero Center, 30ft Floor
San Francisco, CA 94lll-3719
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-03-297
Dear Ms. Balok:
This letter is in response to your request on behalf of Marin Healthcare District
Boardmember Suzanna Coxhead for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions
of the Political Reform Act (the "Act').r The Act requires'the Commission to provide
written advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in question or to that
person's authorized representative. (Section 83114(b); regulation 18329(a) (copy
enclosed).) However, the Commission may decline to provide written advice where the
requestor is seeking advice that requires us to evaluate conduct that has already occurred.
(Regulation 18329(b)(8XA).) In addition, the Commission may also decline to provide
written advice if it would be inappropriate or otherwise not in the public interest to do so.
(Regulation I 8329(b)(8XF).)
Formal written advice is the application of law to a given set of facts provided by
the requestor and has the purpose of conferring immunityto the requestor. (Section
83114; regulation 18329(b)(7).) After a careful review ofttre facts provided, we decline
to provide written advice to you at this time. The s€lme or very similar issues are
currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division.
Please note that our decision is not a determination of the questions posed in your
request. 'In addition, nothirig in the Act prevents the official from seeking legal advice
I Govemment Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the Califomia Code of Regulations.


t
File No. W-03-297
Page No. 2
from you or private counsel. Should you have any questions, please contact the
Commission at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By:
Galena West
Counsel, Legal Division
Enclosure
Gwje

sections.question_synthetic: Boardmember Suzanna Coxhead of Marin Healthcare District sought advice regarding potential conflict-of-interest issues under the Political Reform Act, but the specific questions are not detailed in this declination letter.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide written advice because the same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division, citing regulation 18329(b)(8)(A) which allows the Commission to decline advice regarding conduct that has already occurred.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a declination letter (W-03-297). No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present in the document. The substance of the requestor's questions is not detailed in the letter itself. The letter explains the legal basis for declining and notes that the decision is not a determination on the merits.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Boardmember Suzanna Coxhead of Marin Healthcare District sought advice regarding potential conflict-of-interest issues under the Political Reform Act, but the specific questions are not detailed in this declination letter.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC declined to provide written advice because the same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division, citing regulation 18329(b)(8)(A) which allows the Commission to decline advice regarding conduct that has already occurred.

embedding.summary: This is an FPPC declination letter denying a request for written advice from Boardmember Suzanna Coxhead regarding Political Reform Act conflict-of-interest provisions because similar issues are under active investigation by the Enforcement Division.
